title: Global capability centres are rewriting white-collar India
dek: Over 1,700 GCCs now employ close to two million people. The data shows they are climbing the value chain faster than the IT services giants did.

author.name: Arjun Nair
author.bio: Arjun writes on markets and corporate finance. Before journalism, he spent five years as a sell-side equity analyst.

charts.title: The quiet doubling
charts.subtitle: Estimated GCC employment in India, millions

body.text: While the IT services majors dominate the headlines, the fastest-growing employer of India’s engineering graduates is a category most people cannot name: the global capability centre, the in-house India arm of a multinational.

The salary data is where the story sharpens. Median GCC compensation runs meaningfully above services-industry benchmarks for equivalent roles, and the roles themselves are shifting from support functions toward product engineering, design and research. Hyderabad and Pune are gaining share from Bengaluru — a decentralisation the office-rental data confirms.
